issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class defines a button with min-height 36 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum 44 × 44 px touch target. The vertical padding (var(--space-1-5) ≈ 6 px) is less than the 8 px needed to compensate, so the effective touch area may still be insufficient for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

fpTitles: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size

issuesFound.title: Negative letter‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The heading rule `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6` applies `letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight)`, which resolves to ‑0.025 em. Negative letter‑spacing can make text harder to read for users with low vision or dyslexia, violating WCAG 2.1 1.4.8 (visual presentation).
issuesFound.fix:
Use a non‑negative spacing value (e.g., the neutral token) instead of the negative one.

Line 236:   letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal);

fpTitles: Negative letter‑spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` and only an `outline‑offset`, removing the default focus ring without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for all button variants that inherit this rule. Keyboard users lose the focus cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 36px` (below the 44 px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum) and its vertical padding (`padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5)`) adds only 6 px of padding on each side, which does not compensate to reach the recommended 44 px touch area. Users on touch devices may find the button hard to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Links inside the footer (`footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a`) have `min-height: 24px` with no padding, resulting in a touch target far smaller than the 44 px recommended size, making them difficult to tap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto;` which does **not** hide the element from visual layout while keeping it accessible; instead it leaves the content visible to AT and may be ignored. This defeats the purpose of a screen‑reader‑only utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only-focusable)
issuesFound.explanation: `.sr-only-focusable` repeats the same incorrect properties as `.sr-only`, failing to hide content from AT and not providing the intended “focusable but otherwise hidden” behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: `.sr-only-focusable { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter‑spacing value (--letter-spacing-tighter) impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property `--letter-spacing-tighter` is set to `-0.05em`, a negative letter‑spacing that can make text harder to read, especially for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `--letter-spacing-tighter: 0em;`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter‑spacing value (--letter-spacing-tight) impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property `--letter-spacing-tight` is set to `-0.025em`, another negative letter‑spacing that reduces legibility for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 150: `--letter-spacing-tight: 0em;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` rule applies `transform: translateY(-1px)`, creating a spatial movement on hover. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block disables or overrides this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: translateY(-1px); text-decoration: none; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform on hover)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` rule applies a 3‑D transform (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg);`). Although a reduced‑motion block exists for `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover`, the hover rule still defines a transform that is not explicitly overridden inside the same selector within the reduced‑motion media query, potentially causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` when drawer has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button receives `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the trigger has `aria-expanded` but no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship required for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element always has a unique `id` (e.g. `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), or always set `aria-controls` on the trigger regardless of the drawer’s `id`.

---

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tabs container missing required `role="tablist"`
issuesFound.explanation: The element that groups the product‑filter buttons (`_tabsEl = qs('[data-filter-tabs]')`) is used as a tab interface but lacks `role="tablist"`. Without this role, assistive technologies cannot identify the group as a set of tabs.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `role="tablist"` (and an appropriate `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`) to the container element in the markup or via JavaScript after it is selected.

---

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons missing `role="tab"` and `aria-selected`
issuesFound.explanation: The individual filter buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) act as tabs but are not given `role="tab"` or `aria-selected` attributes. The script only toggles `aria-pressed`, which does not convey tab selection to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Set `role="tab"` on each button and update `aria-selected="true"` for the active button (and `false` for others) when the filter changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tabs container missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The tab list container has no accessible name, so screen‑reader users receive no context for the filter controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` (e.g. `aria-label="Product filter tabs"`) to the container element.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state element not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the current filters, the element `_emptyState` is shown (`removeAttribute('hidden')`) but it has no `role="status"` or `aria-live` attribute, so the change is not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to the empty‑state element (or wrap it in a live region) so that its appearance is announced.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated with the number of visible products, but there is no live‑region announcement of the new count, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge text, set `_countBadge.setAttribute('aria-live','polite')` or update a nearby live region with the new count.

---

issuesFound.title: Sort‑select changes not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order updates the product list but does not provide an audible cue; the sort‑select element lacks an associated live region to announce the new sorting.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and update its text after the sort change (e.g., `"Sorted by price ascending"`). ---

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle buttons lack `aria-pressed` initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) are given `aria-pressed` only when the user clicks them; on page load the button representing the initial view mode does not have `aria-pressed="true"`, causing a mismatch between visual state and ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix:
During initialization, set `aria-pressed="true"` on the button that matches the initial view mode and `false` on the others.

---

issuesFound.title: Search‑clear button missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (`_clearBtn`) is identified only by its visual “×” icon and has no `aria-label`, making its purpose unclear to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-label="Clear search"` to the button element.

---

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list items lack `role="option"` ARIA‑label
issuesFound.explanation: While each suggestion `<li>` has `role="option"` and `aria-selected`, the inner clickable element (`<a>` or `<span>`) does not have an accessible name when the suggestion text is inserted via `innerHTML`. Some screen readers may not read the suggestion text.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the clickable element receives the suggestion text as its `textContent` or set `aria-label` to the same text.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel missing `role="region"` when generated dynamically
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ panel (`panel`) is given `role="region"` only if it does not already have a role. If the markup supplies a custom role (e.g., `role="tabpanel"`), the panel may lack `aria-labelledby` or appropriate labeling.
issuesFound.fix:
Always set `aria-labelledby` on the panel regardless of its existing role, and ensure the panel has `role="region"` or a suitable landmark role.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions not announced via dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ open/close functions call `liveRegion.announce`, which uses a shared live region. If multiple FAQs are opened/closed rapidly, announcements may be overwritten, causing loss of information.
issuesFound.fix:
Use a dedicated live region for FAQ status updates (e.g., `
`) and update it separately for each action.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack `aria-controls` linking to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button gets `aria-controls` only if the drawer element has an `id`. When the drawer has no `id` the button has no `aria-controls`, leaving screen‑reader users without a programmatic relationship between the button and the navigation panel.

issuesFound.title: “Scroll‑to‑top” button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button selected by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is shown/hidden but never receives an `aria-label` or visible text, so assistive technologies cannot convey its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state visibility changes are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, `_updateEmptyState()` shows or hides the empty‑state element, but the element lacks `aria-live`, so the change is silent to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack `aria-label` or `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons created for the carousel are identified only by CSS classes; they have no accessible name, making it impossible for screen‑reader users to know their function.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel pagination element lacks `aria-live` for page changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination text (e.g., “Page 2 of 4”) is updated in `_syncPagination()`, but the container has no `aria-live`, so screen‑reader users are not informed when the page number changes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack an explicit ARIA role
issuesFound.explanation: The panel element for each dropdown is hidden/shown but never receives a role such as `role="menu"` or `role="region"`. Without a role, assistive technologies cannot convey the purpose of the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
When registering a dropdown, set an appropriate role on the panel:  

```js
if (!panel.getAttribute('role')) {
  setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu'); // or 'region' if not a menu
}
```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation overlay does not receive `aria-hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is hidden by removing the `hidden` attribute, but it never gets `aria-hidden="true"` when not visible, so screen readers may still expose it.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header lacks a landmark role (`banner`)
issuesFound.explanation: The header element is used as a site banner but does not have `role="banner"` or `aria-label`, which helps AT identify the region.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button missing `aria-pressed` when JavaScript fails to initialize
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons are given `aria-pressed` only after the module initializes. If initialization fails, the buttons have no `aria-pressed`, leaving their state ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and locks scrolling, but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true". Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know when the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside the open() function after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance missing aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, yet it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not be notified that the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it never updates the question button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true". Users of screen readers cannot detect the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email']); adds live validation, but inlineValidator marks errors only with aria‑describedby and CSS classes; it never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input when invalid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Extend inlineValidator to call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “full_name” field validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The full_name input is bound with inlineValidator.bind() but the validator does not set aria‑invalid when the field fails the required or minLength checks, leaving the error invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Modify inlineValidator to set aria-invalid on the field for any validation failure.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates (product count badge) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the count badge text but does not provide an aria‑live announcement. Users relying on screen readers receive no feedback that the product list changed.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element and update its text with the new count after each filter operation.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips UI changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render() adds or removes chip elements to reflect current filters, but no live‑region is used, so screen‑reader users are unaware of filter changes.
issuesFound.fix: Create an aria‑live region (e.g., <div role="status" aria-live="polite">) and update it with a brief message each time chips are rendered or removed.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list status updates are not announced with aria‑live when the list is hidden due to no results
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.hide() adds hidden to the list but does not update any live region to convey that no suggestions are available, leaving users without feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Update the existing status element (or create one) with a message like “No suggestions found” and ensure it has aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher button does not announce the new theme via live region (only uses liveRegion.announce which may be suppressed if the user has reduced motion)
issuesFound.explanation: themeSwitcher.set() calls liveRegion.announce to inform users of the theme change, but if prefers‑reduced‑motion is true the live region may be suppressed, violating WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the announcement is made through an element with role="status" and aria‑live="assertive" that is not suppressed by reduced‑motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel “play/pause” button does not have aria‑pressed updated on initial load (state defaults to false but attribute not set)
issuesFound.explanation: testimonialsCarousel.init() creates _playBtn but does not set aria‑pressed to "false" until the first user interaction, leaving the button’s state ambiguous for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In init(), after locating _playBtn, call setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); and set an appropriate aria‑label for the paused state.

fpTitles: Contact form “full_name” field validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
Site‑search suggestion list status updates are not announced with aria‑live when the list is hidden due to no results
Theme switcher button does not announce the new theme via live region (only uses liveRegion.announce which may be suppressed if the user has reduced motion)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened (`mobileNav.open`), the code locks scrolling and adds CSS classes but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` state to `true`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function (after the guard check).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may be missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: During `mobileNav.init` the script only sets `aria-controls` on the trigger if the drawer element has an `id`. The drawer (`_drawer`) is obtained from `SELECTORS.navDrawer` or `SELECTORS.primaryNav`, both of which are undefined in the selector map, so the drawer often has no `id` and the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"` in the markup) or, in `mobileNav.init`, generate an `id` for the drawer if missing and then set `aria-controls` accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” routine does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance` adds the `open` CSS class and removes `hidden` from the panel, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `true`. Users of assistive technology cannot detect that the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` right after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu keyboard activation lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is opened via keyboard (Enter, Space, or ArrowDown) the code calls `_openInstance` but does not announce the change to a screen‑reader. The navigation experience is silent for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: After `_openInstance(inst)` in `_onTriggerKeydown`, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` or a similar message.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” routine does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open` adds the `open` CSS class and animates the panel, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `true`. The state of the FAQ item remains hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “close” routine does not announce closure via keyboard Escape
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed with Escape, the code calls `_closeInstance` but does not announce that the menu has been closed. Screen‑reader users are left unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `_closeInstance(inst, true)` in the Escape branch of `_onTriggerKeydown`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” routine does not announce closure
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes CSS classes and unlocks scrolling but does not announce that the navigation menu has been closed. Users relying on a screen reader receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button does not update aria‑pressed state consistently
issuesFound.explanation: `themeSwitcher.toggle` changes the theme but only updates `aria-pressed` on buttons that have a `data-theme-toggle` attribute when they are clicked. If a button is toggled via the keyboard (Space/Enter) the `aria-pressed` state may become out of sync because the handler does not set it. This violates the requirement that toggle buttons reflect their state via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: In `themeSwitcher.toggle` (or the click handler), after changing the theme, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === newPref));` for the button that triggered the change, ensuring the attribute always reflects the current state.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload component does not announce successful file addition
issuesFound.explanation: When a file is added via drag‑and‑drop or the file‑input, the component creates a list entry but does not announce the addition to a live region. Users of assistive technology cannot know that the file was queued for upload.
issuesFound.fix: After `_addFile(binding, file)` adds the entry to the list, call `liveRegion.announce(\`\${file.name} added to upload queue.\`);` to provide an audible confirmation.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger may be missing aria‑controls attribute
File‑upload component does not announce successful file addition

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and locks scrolling but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded state to true, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is shown/hidden without toggling aria‑hidden (visibility‑tree issue)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element _drawer is revealed/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but no aria‑hidden attribute is added or removed, so the element remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); and in close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); to keep the tree in sync.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay is hidden without aria‑hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay _overlay is shown/hidden via CSS class changes, but its aria‑hidden state is never updated, causing hidden overlay content to stay in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add removeAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden'); and in close() add setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.renderResult() adds CSS error classes and inserts an error message but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the invalid field, breaking the required ARIA mapping for form errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch of _renderResult add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); (and ensure the if branch removes it).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, suggestionListRenderer.render() updates the DOM but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware of new suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: After suggestionListRenderer.render() call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${results.length} suggestions`); or a similar message.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu opening is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds CSS classes to display the panel but does not provide any aria‑live announcement, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback that the menu opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${trigger.textContent.trim()} opened`); or emit a custom event that triggers an announcement.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu closing is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() hides the panel without announcing that the menu closed, causing a loss of context for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${trigger.textContent.trim()} closed`);.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name (empty button)
issuesFound.explanation: The button identified by SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn has no inner text, aria‑label, or title attribute, so it is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); or provide visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑controls when drawer has no id (robustness)
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the code only sets aria‑controls if _drawer.id exists; if the drawer lacks an id, the trigger will have no relationship to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer always has an id (e.g., _drawer.id = shortId('nav-drawer');) before setting aria‑controls, or always set aria‑controls to the drawer’s selector.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not receive role="button" when it is not a <button> element
issuesFound.explanation: If _navToggle is a <div> or <span>, it lacks an explicit role and may not be announced as a button.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() add if (!['BUTTON','A'].includes(_trigger.tagName)) setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button'); and ensure tabindex is set.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is shown/hidden without toggling aria‑hidden (visibility‑tree issue)
Mobile navigation overlay is hidden without aria‑hidden management
Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name (empty button)
Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑controls when drawer has no id (robustness)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance in the dropdown menu controller toggles the visibility of a panel but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are given aria‑controls but never receive an aria‑expanded attribute on load, so their collapsed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each FaqItem in faqRegistry.parse, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') unless the item is initially open.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, _productFilter._updateCountBadge updates the numeric badge but does not write to a live‑region, so users of assistive technology are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and set its textContent to “X products found” each time the badge updates.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips render not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips.render function updates the list of chips without notifying a live‑region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of added or removed filter chips.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, update a dedicated <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> with a summary such as “Filter “Free” added” or “Filter “Free” removed”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce change
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period via pricing.setPeriod updates the UI but does not announce the new period to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function marks fields as invalid by adding an error message element but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email address but only displays an error message; it does not set aria‑invalid on the <input> element, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: After a validation failure, call setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s inline validation adds error messages but does not set aria‑invalid on the corresponding inputs, leaving the error state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation routine for each field, set setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when invalid and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels hidden with CSS class only (no aria‑hidden)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code removes the open class but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the panel remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _dropdownMenu._closeInstance, after removing the open class, call setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove the attribute when opening.

fpTitles: Newsletter form email field missing aria‑invalid on error
Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions add/remove CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never update `aria-expanded` on the `.nav-toggle` button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing the mobile navigation drawer.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria-controls` link to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button controls the navigation drawer (`#nav-menu`), but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` or when the trigger is first cached, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', '#nav-menu')` (or the drawer’s id) so the control is properly described.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code only adds CSS classes and removes `hidden` attributes, but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation errors do not set `aria-invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: `newsletterForm` uses `inlineValidator` which marks fields with error classes and inserts error messages, but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid `<input>` elements, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()` or `inlineValidator._renderResult()`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when the field is invalid and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation errors do not set `aria-invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form also relies on `inlineValidator`, which does not apply `aria-invalid` to fields that fail validation, leaving screen‑reader users without error state information.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 4 – ensure `aria-invalid="true"` is set on invalid inputs and cleared on valid ones.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update `aria-expanded` when panels open/close
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` toggle visibility and CSS classes but never modify the `aria-expanded` state on the question button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing module updates prices without live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` changes the displayed price values on all plan cards but does not announce the change to screen readers, so users are unaware of the new billing amount.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each price, call `liveRegion.announce()` with a concise message, e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to annual billing – $99 per year')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates product count badge without live‑region feedback
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._updateCountBadge()` updates the numeric badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of how many products are shown after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)` or update an existing `aria-live` region.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher does not announce theme changes to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: `themeSwitcher.set()` applies a `data-theme` attribute and CSS classes but does not announce the change, so users cannot know the theme has switched.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the theme, call `liveRegion.announce('Theme changed to dark mode')` or similar.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not set `aria-controls` when the trigger lacks it
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()`, the code adds `aria-controls` only if missing, but the attribute value is set to the panel’s generated `id`. However, the panel may not have an `id` if the developer omitted it, leading to a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel always receives an `id` (e.g., via `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')`) before assigning `aria-controls` to the trigger, guaranteeing a valid reference.

fpTitles: Contact form validation errors do not set `aria-invalid`
Dropdown menu does not set `aria-controls` when the trigger lacks it

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button class="nav-toggle">` used to open/close the mobile drawer never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the navigation is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`; and in `mobileNav.toggle()` update the attribute accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button controls the drawer (`#nav-drawer`), but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer element is identified, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` (ensure the drawer has an `id`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened (`_openInstance`), the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` state is never set to `true`.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed, the trigger’s `aria-expanded` remains unchanged, leaving the state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_closeInstance(inst, …)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown does not reference the panel it controls, so assistive tech cannot associate them.
issuesFound.fix: When registering a dropdown (`register(trigger, panel)`) ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` where `panelId` is the panel’s `id`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` to `true`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is collapsed, the trigger’s `aria-expanded` remains `true`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close(item)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`data-period`) toggle visual state with a CSS class but never reflect the selected state via `aria‑pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑controls to price panel
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons control the price display panels but lack an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each period button has `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', panelId)` where `panelId` is the id of the element showing the price for that period.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` changes the visible count but does not provide an audible cue; users of screen readers are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} found`)` or update an `aria-live` region tied to the badge.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus(count, isError)` changes the status element’s text but the element lacks `aria-live="polite"` (or the update is not sent to a live region), so screen‑reader users do not hear loading/error messages.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite')` and call `liveRegion.announce(_statusEl.textContent)` after each status change.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons do not set aria‑pressed when selected
issuesFound.explanation: `_syncTabAriaStates()` toggles a CSS class on filter tabs but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving the selected state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_syncTabAriaStates()` after toggling the class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))`.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria‑controls to their panels
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs control the product grid but lack an `aria-controls` reference, so assistive tech cannot associate the tab with the content it shows.
issuesFound.fix: When initializing tabs, assign each button an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the id of the product grid (`_gridEl.id`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close functions do not set aria‑expanded on the trigger (duplicate of Issue 1 but for the separate `open()`/`close()` methods)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` methods manipulate the drawer but never update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close functions do not set aria‑controls on the trigger (duplicate of Issue 2)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button never receives an `aria-controls` attribute referencing the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an `id` and call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` in both `open()` and `close()`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close function does not remove aria‑expanded when hiding panel (duplicate of Issue 4)
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-expanded` attribute remains `true` after a dropdown is closed, misleading AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` before hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ close function does not remove aria‑expanded when collapsing answer (duplicate of Issue 7)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria-expanded` stays `true` after the answer is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` before hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header scroll‑hide/show does not announce state changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides or shows on scroll, no live‑region announcement is made, so users of AT are unaware of the UI change.
issuesFound.fix: After adding/removing the `headerHidden` class, call `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden')` or `liveRegion.announce('Header shown')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay click‑outside handler does not set aria‑hidden on the overlay when it is hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay is hidden by adding a CSS class but its `aria-hidden` attribute is never updated, leaving it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding the overlay, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing, set to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hide/show does not manage aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden via `hidden` attribute but when they are shown the `aria-hidden` attribute is not removed, causing duplicate hidden state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` after removing `hidden`, also call `removeAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden')`; in `_closeInstance` after adding `hidden`, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) acts as a binary toggle but never receives `aria‑pressed` to convey its state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` show or hide a dropdown panel but never modify `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving assistive technology unaware of the panel state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the code only adds `aria‑haspopup` but does not ensure `aria‑controls` points to the panel’s id, breaking the relationship for AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not announce state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` toggle the panel visibility but never use a live region to announce “question opened” or “question closed”. Users of screen readers miss the state change.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑describedby to error message
issuesFound.explanation: Although an error `<span>` is created, the field is never linked via `aria-describedby`, so screen readers do not read the error text.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `errorEl`, call `srUtils.addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` (already exists in `srUtils.markInvalid`). Ensure `markInvalid` is invoked:  
```js
if (!result.valid) {
  srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);   // <-- add
}
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) changes visual state but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving AT unaware of the current period.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick` (or `_syncToggleState`) add:  
```js
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false'); // <-- add
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `data-period` act as mutually exclusive toggles but only receive a CSS “active” class; they never receive `aria-pressed` to convey state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not provide a live‑region announcement, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating all plan cards in `setPeriod`, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(
  period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Annual billing selected'
                    : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Monthly billing selected'
);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not announce to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile drawer changes focus but never announces the change, so AT users may not know the menu is now visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
At the end of `open()` and `close()`:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');   // in open()
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClose || 'Navigation menu closed'); // in close()
```  

These twelve issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) for the provided script.

fpTitles: Inline validator does not set aria‑describedby to error message

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (mobileNav open/close)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav object’s open() and close() functions change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in close() (e.g., Line 1159: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', true);` and Line 1170: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', false);`).

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (state not announced)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (SELECTORS.navToggle) is a <button> that switches between two states (open/closed) but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving assistive technologies without a state cue.
issuesFound.fix: In the same open() and close() functions add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false') respectively (e.g., Line 1155 and Line 1175).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick (and the corresponding _openInstance/​_closeInstance functions) the visibility of the dropdown panel is toggled, but the trigger button never receives an aria‑expanded attribute update.
issuesFound.fix: Inside _ openInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and inside _ closeInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') (e.g., Line 1382 and Line 1401).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed (state not announced)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger <button> acts as a two‑state control (open/closed) but never sets aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive its state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ onTriggerClick or the open/close helpers, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false') when closing (e.g., Line 1375 and Line 1395).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds the CSS.open class and animates the panel, but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') (e.g., Line 2102). Ensure the corresponding close() already sets 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing aria‑pressed (state not announced)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons act as toggles but never receive aria‑pressed, so their expanded/collapsed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.toggle() or the open/close functions, add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') on open and 'false' on close (e.g., Line 2095 and Line 2108).

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() calls srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl) which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid='true'. Assistive technologies therefore do not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') (e.g., modify Line 2543). Likewise, in srUtils.markValid remove the attribute (or set to 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dynamic UI updates in product filter are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the product count badge and empty‑state message, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count, call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${total} products`) or similar (e.g., add after Line 1768). Also announce when the empty‑state appears/disappears (after Line 1775).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updated price to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates price numbers in the DOM but never writes to a live region, leaving AT users unaware of the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix: After the loop that calls _updatePlanCard add liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${period} billing`) (e.g., after Line 2470).

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button receives aria‑controls but never gets an initial aria‑expanded='false' value, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their collapsed state on load.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') (e.g., Line 1150).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls attribute on init
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but not aria‑controls pointing to the submenu panel, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register() after creating panelId, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId) (e.g., Line 1368).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced via a dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.render() populates the suggestion list but only updates a hidden status element; no dedicated live‑region message is sent for each new suggestion set, so AT users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${suggestions.length} suggestions`) (e.g., after Line 1245).

issuesFound.title: User actions that change visible content (e.g., product filter, pricing toggle, FAQ open/close) lack dedicated live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: These actions modify the UI but do not write to a unique aria‑live region, violating JS‑G.
issuesFound.fix:
For each action, add a liveRegion.announce() call that describes the specific change (e.g., after Line 1768, 2470, 2102).  

These issue blocks reference the exact functions, selectors, and line numbers identified in the source file and satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input aria-describedby references a non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email includes aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id is rendered, so screen readers cannot locate the error message. This affects users who rely on assistive technology to understand validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id (or remove the aria-describedby if not needed).  

  Line 1158: ```<TextInput  
    id="newsletter-email"  
    type="email"  
    name="email"  
    placeholder="you@company.com"  
    autoComplete="email"  
    value={email.state.value}  
    onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}  
    onBlur={email.onBlur}  
    required  
    aria-required="true"  
    invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}  
    aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}  
    />```  

  Insert after the <TextInput> inside the <FormGroup> the following:  

  Line 1165: ```<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} />```  

---

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input aria-describedby references a non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” includes aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but no element with that id is rendered, leaving AT users without error feedback.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea aria-describedby references a non‑existent element

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button does not communicate loading state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the newsletter submission receives a loading={isSubmitting} prop, but the Button component expects isLoading. Consequently aria‑busy is never set and no spinner appears, leaving AT users unaware that the form is processing.

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button does not communicate loading state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the contact form submission receives a loading={isSubmitting} prop, but the component expects isLoading. As a result aria‑busy is not set and no loading spinner is shown, so AT users cannot detect that the submission is in progress.
issuesFound.fix:
Rename the prop to isLoading.  

  Line 1502: ```<Button  
    type="submit"  
    variant="primary"  
    size="md"  
    loading={isSubmitting}  
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}  
  >```  

  Replace with:  

  Line 1502: ```<Button  
    type="submit"  
    variant="primary"  
    size="md"  
    isLoading={isSubmitting}  
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}  
  >```

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) are toggled via click listener in `productFilter.init`. If these elements are not native `<button>` tags, they lack a keyboard handler for Enter/Space activation. `createRovingTabindex` handles arrow navigation but not activation, and the click listener does not have a corresponding keydown handler.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2085: _tabsEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onTabClick(e); } });

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts or declines cookies, the banner hides and consent state changes, but no message is announced to screen readers via `liveRegion.announce`. Users are not informed of the successful action.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3230: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.consentAccepted || 'Cookie preferences saved.'); (Add to _onAccept and _onDecline)

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat numbers are updated dynamically via `animateNumber` when scrolled into view, but the new values are not announced to screen readers. This content change is not conveyed to non-visual users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3130: onComplete: () => { el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg); el.setAttribute('aria-label', el.textContent); liveRegion.announce(`Stat updated to ${el.textContent}`); }

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input is validated in `_onApply`, and error messages are shown in `_statusEl`, but `aria-invalid="true"` is not set on the input element (`_inputEl`) when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2555: if (!result.valid) { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); } else { _inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }

Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module update the `aria-expanded` attribute and visibility of dropdown panels but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened" or "Menu closed") to the `liveRegion`. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes via keyboard or mouse.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1670: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` (add inside `_openInstance` after setting attributes)
  Line 1684: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` (add inside `_closeInstance` after setting attributes)

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner visibility not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in the `cookieConsent` module displays the consent dialog (often triggered by the user clicking "Manage cookie settings") but does not announce its appearance to the `liveRegion`. Users relying on screen readers may not realize the dialog has appeared if focus management is delayed or if they are not monitoring the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2725: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie settings dialog opened');` (add inside `_showBanner` after appending to body)

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle lacks keyboard activation for non-native elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher.init` function attaches a `click` listener to `[data-theme-toggle]` elements. If these elements are not native `<button>` or `<a>` tags (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` with `role="button"`), keyboard users cannot activate them using Enter or Space keys because no `keydown` handler is registered for these keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2877: `document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });` (add alongside click listener)

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks keyboard activation for non-native elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop.init` function attaches a `click` listener to `_btn` (`#scroll-top-btn`). If this element is not a native `<button>` or `<a>`, keyboard users cannot activate it using Enter or Space keys as no `keydown` handler is registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1528: `_btn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onBtnKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });` (add alongside click listener)

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel navigation buttons lack keyboard activation for non-native elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.init` function attaches `click` listeners to `_prevBtn` and `_nextBtn`. If these elements are not native `<button>` tags, keyboard users cannot activate them using Enter or Space keys because no `keydown` handler is registered for these specific buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2293: `_prevBtn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onPrevKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); pause(); prev(); } });`
  Line 2294: `_nextBtn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onNextKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); pause(); next(); } });`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard navigation buttons lack keyboard activation for non-native elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard.init` function attaches `click` listeners to `_prevBtn` and `_nextBtn`. If these elements are not native `<button>` tags, keyboard users cannot activate them using Enter or Space keys because no `keydown` handler is registered for these specific buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2565: `_prevBtn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onPrevKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onPrev(); } });`
  Line 2566: `_nextBtn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onNextKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onNext(); } });`

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput apply button lacks keyboard activation for non-native elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `discountCodeInput.init` function attaches a `click` listener to `_applyBtn`. If this element is not a native `<button>` tag, keyboard users cannot activate it using Enter or Space keys because no `keydown` handler is registered for this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2177: `_applyBtn.addEventListener('keydown', function _onApplyKeydown(e) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onApply(); } });` (add alongside click listener)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space activation in keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion._onKeydown` function handles Arrow, Home, and End keys for navigation between FAQ triggers but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the focused trigger. Keyboard users can navigate to a trigger but cannot expand/collapse it without a mouse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935: `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); const item = faqRegistry.getAll().find(i => i.trigger === trigger); if (item) toggle(item); break;` (add to switch statement in `_onKeydown`)

issuesFound.title: createRovingTabindex utility lacks Enter/Space activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `createRovingTabindex` function handles Arrow, Home, and End keys for moving focus within a group but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate the focused item. This affects `productFilter` tabs and `pricing` period buttons if they are not native buttons, preventing keyboard activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); setActive(items[idx], true); break;` (add to switch statement in `_onKeydown` inside `createRovingTabindex`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger lacks Enter/Space activation in keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav._onTriggerKeydown` function handles the Escape key to close the menu but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the menu open/closed. If the trigger (`.nav-toggle`) is not a native `<button>`, keyboard users cannot open the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); toggle(); }` (add to `_onTriggerKeydown` function)

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input validation function `_onApply` announces error messages via live region when a code is invalid, but it fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element itself. Screen reader users may hear the error announcement but the input field does not carry the programmatic invalid state required for WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2676: if (result.valid) { ... } else { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(result.message || 'Invalid code'); }

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the `is-open` CSS class from the drawer element to hide it visually, but it does not explicitly set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer. If the CSS uses `visibility`, `opacity`, or `transform` instead of `display: none`, the navigation drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1642: removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._onAccept` function saves the user's consent preference and hides the banner, but it does not announce a confirmation message (e.g., "Preferences saved") to screen readers. Users may not be aware the action was successfully registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3366: function _onAccept() { _store(ACCEPTED); analytics.grantConsent(); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.'); _hideBanner(); ... }

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard.init` function sets up navigation buttons (`_prevBtn`, `_nextBtn`) to switch between wizard steps (panels), but it does not set `aria-controls` on these buttons pointing to the ID of the active step panel. This breaks the relationship between the trigger and the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3085: if (_prevBtn) { setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current - 1].panelEl.id); ... } if (_nextBtn) { setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current + 1].panelEl.id); ... }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator._animate` function updates the text content of statistic elements (e.g., "10,000+ clients") dynamically via JavaScript animation. It updates `aria-label` on completion but does not use a live region to announce the change, so screen reader users may miss the updated values.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3207: onComplete: () => { el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg); el.setAttribute('aria-label', el.textContent); liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${el.textContent}`); },

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module hides the drawer but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has closed. The open function announces, but the close action is silent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the drawer visibility using CSS classes (navOpenClass) but does not toggle aria-hidden on the _drawer element. If the drawer is visually hidden via CSS (e.g. off-canvas) rather than display:none, it remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1365: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1385: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open and close actions not announced via live region

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1635: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1648: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to screen readers

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher click handler lacks keyboard support for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher module attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not attach a keydown listener for Enter/Space. If these elements are not native buttons in the HTML, keyboard users cannot activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3755: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === 'Enter' || ev.key === ' ') { ev.preventDefault(); _onThemeClick(ev); } });

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle click handler lacks keyboard support for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _onViewToggle function in the productFilter module is bound to click events on [data-view-mode] elements via delegation. No keydown handler is provided for Enter/Space. If these elements are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot toggle the view mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'keydown', function _onViewToggleKey(e) { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onViewToggle(e); } });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer to visible but fails to update the trigger button's aria-expanded state to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", causing state desynchronization for screen reader users who rely on this attribute to know if the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The paired _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving assistive technology with incorrect state information when the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but omits setting the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close function sets it to "false", resulting in a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid removes aria-invalid instead of setting false
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function clears the invalid state by removing the aria-invalid attribute entirely. While absence implies validity, explicitly setting aria-invalid="false" is more robust for dynamic forms to ensure assistive technology reliably announces the state change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state. This affects all forms using inlineValidator (newsletter, contact, wizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1382: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }
