issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes them difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are sized at 20px by 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may miss these small targets when trying to toggle form options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1418: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, making content visible in the layout and potentially hiding it from screen readers depending on browser. It fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 371: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link element transitions transform properties without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the sliding animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: .skip-link { position: fixed; top: var(--space-3); left: var(--space-3); z-index: var(--z-toast); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); border: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); transform: translateY(-200%); transition: transform var(--transition-base); white-space: nowrap; } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons transition transform (translateY on active) without a reduced-motion override. Users sensitive to motion may be affected by the button press animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 567: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button transitions transform (translateY on hover/active) without respecting prefers-reduced-motion. This can cause discomfort for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 888: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); min-height: 32px; border-radius: var(--radius-xl); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which tightens text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word spacing can cause words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses #ee9999 (light red) text on #ffffff (white) background, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 1.5:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text and 3:1 for large text, making it unreadable for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 612: .btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration without providing a non-colour visual differentiator for body-text links. Users with colour blindness cannot distinguish links from surrounding text when colour is the only cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 293: a { text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group textarea:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails in the _addFile function, the code announces the error via liveRegion.announceAssertive but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element (binding.inputEl). Additionally, the error message is not associated with the input via aria-describedby. Screen reader users will not know the field is invalid or understand the error context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5370: Add setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and srUtils.addDescribedBy(binding.inputEl, errorMessageElement); when validation fails. Also ensure aria-invalid is cleared to 'false' or removed when validation passes or files are cleared.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is displayed in _statusEl and announced, but the input field (_inputEl) is not marked with aria-invalid="true" and the error message element is not linked to the input via aria-describedby. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the invalid field and its associated error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5120: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and srUtils.addDescribedBy(_inputEl, _statusEl); when validation fails. Clear aria-invalid (set to 'false' or remove) when validation passes or when clear() is called.

fpTitles: fileUploadField validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer implements a modal dialog pattern (traps focus, blocks interaction with the rest of the page) but does not set aria-modal="true" on the drawer element. Screen reader users may not be informed that the content behind the drawer is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` within the open() function, and correspondingly remove it in close().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but may remain in the accessibility tree if positioned off-screen (e.g., via transform: translateX). The code does not set aria-hidden="true" to ensure the drawer is completely hidden from assistive technologies when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in open() add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user-controllable pause
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout (setTimeout) without a mechanism to pause, stop, or hide them. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) for users who may need more time to read the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Add pause on hover and focus that clears the timeout, and resumes on mouseleave/blur. Line 2623: Replace the setTimeout with a pausable timer mechanism that respects user interaction.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user-controllable pause

issuesFound.title: Keyboard navigation failure in dropdown menu when open
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger's keydown handler only moves focus to the first panel item when the dropdown is closed (opening it). If the dropdown is already open and focus is on the trigger, pressing ArrowDown does nothing. Per the ARIA menu pattern, ArrowDown should always move focus into the menu or to the next item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770-1778: Remove the `if (!inst.open)` condition for the ArrowDown/Enter/Space cases, or add an `else` clause to move focus to the first item when already open.

issuesFound.title: Invalid ARIA attribute on pricing period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets `aria-selected` on the billing period buttons. The `aria-selected` attribute is only valid on elements with roles gridcell, option, row, or tab. Standard buttons (or elements without an explicit tab role) must use `aria-pressed` for toggle state, not `aria-selected`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2677: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and rely solely on `aria-pressed` (or if these are truly tabs, add `role="tab"` to the buttons in the HTML/JS and use `aria-selected` instead of `aria-pressed`).

issuesFound.title: Redundant/conflicting ARIA states on pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets both `aria-pressed` and `aria-selected` on the same elements. These attributes have different meanings and should not be used together. This creates conflicting state information for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2676-2677: Use only one of the two attributes. If the buttons act as a tab list, use `aria-selected` with `role="tab"`. If they act as toggle buttons, use only `aria-pressed`.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls initialization check in pricing module
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module discovers period buttons (`_periodBtns`) but does not programmatically ensure they have `aria-controls` attributes pointing to the corresponding price display panels. While the billing period toggle is not a traditional expand/collapse, associating the control with the affected price region via `aria-controls` improves context for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2645-2650: After discovering `_periodBtns`, add logic to set `aria-controls` on each button to reference the ID of the price display container it updates (e.g., `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-cards-container')`).

issuesFound.title: Focus loss potential in active filter chips
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is removed via click, the chip element is removed from the DOM without explicitly moving focus to a logical location (such as the previous chip, the filter button, or the results). This can cause focus to be lost to the document body, forcing screen reader users to navigate from the top.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2427: After `productFilter.setFilter()` or `removeTag()` is called and the chip is removed from the DOM, set focus to the remaining chip's previous sibling, the filter tab that corresponds to the removed filter, or the product grid container using `focus()`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panel missing aria-labelledby association
issuesFound.explanation: While the wizard steps are assigned `role="tabpanel"`, the code does not explicitly ensure each panel has an `aria-labelledby` attribute referencing the step's title element or trigger. This reduces context for screen reader users navigating between steps.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3168: After parsing the step, ensure the panel has an `aria-labelledby` attribute: `setAttr(panelEl, 'aria-labelledby', srUtils.ensureId(qs('[data-step-title]', panelEl) || panelEl, 'step-title'));`.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-controls for file list
issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone acts as a button that reveals/updates a file list, but it lacks an `aria-controls` attribute associating it with the list element (`listEl`). This helps screen reader users understand which content is being controlled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3303: After creating or discovering `listEl`, add `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(listEl, 'upload-list'));`.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation lacks reduced motion check for aria-label updates
issuesFound.explanation: When reduced motion is preferred, the stat counter immediately sets the final text content but does not explicitly set the `aria-label` attribute on the element. While the text content is updated, ensuring the `aria-label` matches helps maintain consistency for assistive technologies that may rely on the label attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3527: Add `el.setAttribute('aria-label', _format(cfg.target, cfg));` inside the reduced motion block to ensure the accessible name is set immediately.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher sets the active theme on initialization but does not explicitly set `aria-pressed="true"` on the currently active theme toggle button (if they are implemented as toggle buttons) or `aria-checked` (if implemented as radio buttons). This leaves the toggle state ambiguous to screen reader users on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3638: After determining `_current`, query the active toggle button and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` (or `aria-checked` if using radio buttons).

issuesFound.title: Media player controller missing pause announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user presses 'k' or Space to toggle play/pause, the code announces "Playing" or "Paused". However, if the media is paused via the native controls (not the keyboard shortcut), this announcement is not triggered, potentially leaving screen reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3680: Add event listeners for 'play' and 'pause' events on the media element itself to trigger the announcement: `media.addEventListener('play', () => _announce(media, STRINGS.mediaPlaying || 'Playing'));` and similarly for 'pause'.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button scrolls the page to the top (or to the skip link), but it lacks an `aria-controls` attribute indicating that it controls the `main` content or the document body. While not strictly required for a page scroll action, it improves semantic clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1948: Add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-controls', SELECTORS.main.slice(1) || 'main-content');` (assuming the main content has an ID).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu items missing aria-current for active page
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu implementation handles keyboard navigation and focus management, but does not provide a mechanism to indicate which menu item represents the current page (using `aria-current="page"`). This makes it difficult for screen reader users to identify their current location within the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780-1806: In the `_onPanelKeydown` handler or during initialization, check if any link in the panel has an `href` matching `window.location.pathname`, and if so, set `setAttr(link, 'aria-current', 'page')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter sort select missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The sort select control updates the product grid dynamically, but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute associating it with the grid container. This helps screen reader users understand that changing the sort order will update the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2283: Add `if (_sortSelect && _gridEl) setAttr(_sortSelect, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id || srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid'));`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ search input missing aria-controls for results
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ search input filters the list of items, but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the FAQ list container. This association helps screen reader users understand that the input controls the visibility of the list items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2983: Ensure the search input has `aria-controls` set to the FAQ list container ID: `setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_containerEl, 'faq-list'));` (already partially done, but verify it points to the list, not just the container).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button missing aria-controls for status
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form submit button triggers a status update in `_statusEl`, but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute linking it to the status message container. This helps associate the action with its result.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2547: Add `if (_statusEl && submitBtn) setAttr(submitBtn, 'aria-controls', _statusEl.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button missing aria-controls for error summary
issuesFound.explanation: Similar to the newsletter form, the contact form submit button should have `aria-controls` pointing to the error summary container (`_errorSummaryEl`) to associate the submission action with the error feedback region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2607: Add `if (_errorSummaryEl && submitBtn) setAttr(submitBtn, 'aria-controls', _errorSummaryEl.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard navigation buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard's previous, next, and submit buttons control the visibility of step panels, but do not have `aria-controls` attributes pointing to the step container or specific panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3147-3149: Add `aria-controls` to each button referencing the `_stepsContainer` or specific step panel IDs.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner missing aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent dialog has `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title, but lacks `aria-describedby` pointing to the description paragraph. This ensures the description is announced along with the title when the dialog opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3573: Add `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-describedby', 'cookie-banner-desc');` (assuming the description has this ID, or ensure it does).

issuesFound.title: Toast container missing aria-relevant configuration
issuesFound.explanation: The toast container has `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="false"`, but lacks `aria-relevant`. While defaults are acceptable, explicitly setting `aria-relevant="additions"` ensures that only new toasts are announced, not removals.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1046: Add `setAttr(container, 'aria-relevant', 'additions');`.

issuesFound.title: Live region buffers missing aria-hidden during initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The live region creates buffer spans for double-buffering announcements. These spans should have `aria-hidden="true"` to ensure they are not exposed to the accessibility tree as separate text nodes, even though they are visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180-1183: Add `setAttr(nodeA, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and `setAttr(nodeB, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` for both polite and assertive buffers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and updates internal state, but it never sets the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will continue to report the menu as collapsed even when it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line _open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but it fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true", leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1342: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel using animateHeight and removes the hidden attribute, but it never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true", causing screen readers to announce the panel as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3084: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not set aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses the FAQ panel and sets item.open = false, but it does not set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false", leaving the accessibility tree out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3103: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); immediately after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When field validation fails, the _renderResult function adds error classes and displays the error message, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2852: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); immediately after srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); and before addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons indicating the active category filter, but _syncTabAriaStates only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2446: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the hamburger trigger button instead of moving to the first focusable element within the drawer or the drawer itself, requiring additional keyboard navigation to reach the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: Add const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (first) first.focus(); before the emit call;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. This can leave keyboard focus at the bottom of the page or in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: Add _trigger.focus(); at the end of the function before the closing brace;

issuesFound.title: contactForm success message does not receive focus
issuesFound.explanation: After successful form submission, the success status message is displayed but focus is not programmatically moved to it. Screen reader users may not be aware that the submission was successful.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3540: Add _statusEl.focus(); immediately after _statusEl.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm success message does not receive focus
issuesFound.explanation: After successful newsletter subscription, the success message is displayed but focus is not moved to the status element, leaving screen reader users unaware of the success state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3390: Add _statusEl.focus(); immediately after _statusEl.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The close function deactivates the focus trap and removes CSS classes but does not announce the closed state via the live region, so screen reader users are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); before the emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce open/close state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu opens and closes without announcing the state change to screen readers via the live region, making it difficult for users to know when the dropdown is available or dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1347: Add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); in _openInstance. Line 1362: Add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); in _closeInstance before the emit call;

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name check
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM, but if the element lacks text content or an aria-label, it will not have an accessible name for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: Add if (_btn && !_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing accessible name check
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected via SELECTORS.searchClear, but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name if it uses an icon without text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2187: Add if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel lacks pause on page visibility change
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-plays but does not pause when the document is hidden (visibilitychange), violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) for moving content that is not essential.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3248: Add document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => { if (document.hidden) pause(); else if (_playing) play(); }); inside the init function after setting up other listeners;

issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible but fails to update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, leaving users unaware that the navigation is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2848: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2988: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4128: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation helper marks fields as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true". Screen readers will not identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4679: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false"
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the aria-invalid attribute entirely. While this is technically valid, explicitly setting aria-invalid="false" is more robust for screen reader compatibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4689: Replace `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` with `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class toggling but is not explicitly removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden="true" when closed. This can cause screen readers to access off-screen content. Conversely, when opened, aria-hidden should be removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2860: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` Line 2852: `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons lack aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update pricing display across plan cards but do not have aria-controls attributes indicating which price display elements they control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3852: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-cards-container');` (ensure the container has id="pricing-cards-container" or use srUtils.ensureId)

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button lacks aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button controls the carousel rotation but lacks an aria-controls attribute referencing the carousel track or root element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4440: `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-controls', _rootEl.id || srUtils.ensureId(_rootEl, 'testimonials-carousel'));`

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle buttons lack aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons change the color scheme of the document but do not indicate via aria-controls that they control the root html element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5040: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'document-root');` (where the root html element has id="document-root" or use srUtils.ensureId on document.documentElement)

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard navigation buttons lack aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the wizard navigate between steps but lack aria-controls attributes pointing to the step panels they control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4740: `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current].panelEl.id);` and `setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current + 1].panelEl.id);` (update dynamically in _goToStep)

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone lacks aria-controls for file list
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload drop zone controls the display of the selected files list but does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the list container.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4840: `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-controls', listEl.id || srUtils.ensureId(listEl, 'upload-list'));`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner buttons lack aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The accept and decline buttons in the cookie consent banner control the visibility of the banner itself but lack aria-controls attributes referencing the banner element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4940: `setAttr(acceptBtn, 'aria-controls', _bannerEl.id);` and `setAttr(declineBtn, 'aria-controls', _bannerEl.id);`

fpTitles: srUtils.markValid does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false"
mobileNav drawer lacks aria-hidden when closed
testimonialsCarousel play button lacks aria-controls attribute
themeSwitcher toggle buttons lack aria-controls attribute
multiStepWizard navigation buttons lack aria-controls attribute
fileUploadField zone lacks aria-controls for file list
cookieConsent banner buttons lack aria-controls attribute

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function adds CSS classes and emits an event but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has expanded. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2837: After `_open = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds an open class, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Only the close function sets the attribute to "false", leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3004: After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the function animates the panel and updates state, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", so the expanded state is never communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4218: After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2594: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap created but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: A focus trap is instantiated during initialization (line 2910) but is never activated when the navigation opens. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when open, allowing users to tab to background elements and violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2837: After `_lockScroll();`, add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button lacks guaranteed accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (X) for the search input is referenced from the DOM, but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML omits these, screen reader users will hear only "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3522: After `if (_clearBtn)`, add `_clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle uses aria-selected on non-tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons use aria-selected to indicate the active state, but they are not marked with role="tab" and the container is not confirmed to have role="tablist". For toggle buttons that switch between two states (monthly/annual), aria-pressed is the correct attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4144: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` and ensure buttons have `role="button"` (native buttons) or add `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'tab');` if implementing a tablist pattern.

fpTitles: siteSearch clear button lacks guaranteed accessible name

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element, preventing screen reader users from understanding which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer'));` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating a focus trap, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the drawer while it is open. The code references _trap but never initializes or activates it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2057: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. The register function sets the initial value, but it is never updated during toggle operations.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2203: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2215: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions for FAQ items animate the panels and update classes but do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2553: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in open function after `item.open = true;`
  Line 2582: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in close function after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the _renderResult function adds error styling and aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Valid fields also do not have aria-invalid cleared to "false", so screen readers cannot convey the validation state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3452: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`
  Line 3458: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle the billing period, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2970: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices updated to ${period} billing`);` after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item reveals content but does not announce the expansion or the content to screen readers, requiring users to manually navigate to discover the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2578: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || `${item.question} expanded`);` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state change to screen readers, making it difficult for users to know when a menu is available or dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2207: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance
  Line 2225: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1784: After `inst.open = true;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 1800: After `inst.open = false;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened via the open() function, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Although the close() function sets it to "false", the missing "true" value in open() leaves the state inaccurate for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2474: After `item.open = true;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion keyboard handler missing Enter/Space toggle
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers that are not native button elements rely on the _onKeydown handler for keyboard accessibility. The handler captures Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation, but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the item's expanded state. Users cannot activate the accordion with standard keyboard controls.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility functions markInvalid() and markValid() update the aria-describedby association and CSS classes, but never set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1164: In markInvalid(), after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line 1174: In markValid(), at the end of the function add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_syncPagination(totalMatches);` add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    totalMatches === 0 
      ? (STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters')
      : (STRINGS.filterResults || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`)
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name via aria-label or text content. If the button contains only an icon, screen readers will announce it as "button" with no context.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions omit aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels are opened or closed, the code fails to synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the panel’s visibility state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions omit aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion expands and collapses answer panels using animateHeight, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never toggled, preventing screen readers from announcing the current state.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the code visually marks it as invalid and links the error message via aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers rely on this attribute to identify invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation lacks aria-invalid enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s submit handler relies on inlineValidator.validate(), which fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields. Consequently, screen readers cannot identify which specific fields have errors when the form is submitted.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation lacks aria-invalid enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email field using inlineValidator, but because inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid, screen readers cannot convey the error state to users when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step validation lacks aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Per-step validation in the wizard uses inlineValidator, which does not set aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields within wizard steps when users attempt to advance.

issuesFound.title: pricing module does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the displayed prices visually, but screen readers receive no notification of the change via a live region.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips does not announce chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: When users remove a filter chip, the UI updates immediately but no announcement confirms the removal or the remaining active filters to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, violating the requirement that expandable widgets indicate their initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" before the suggestion list is opened, leaving screen readers unaware of the collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but they do not use aria-pressed to communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed to reflect the current theme state on page load, preventing screen readers from identifying which theme is active.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated on open
issuesFound.explanation: Although a focus trap is created during initialization, it is never activated when the menu opens, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer and move to background elements.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField progress not announced
issuesFound.explanation: During file upload, the progress bar updates visually, but the percentage progress is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware of upload status.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent dialog is created with role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is required for modal dialogs to prevent screen readers from accessing background content while the banner is open.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the markValid function removes the error description but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute, potentially leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [_openInstance]: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line [_closeInstance]: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion opens and collapses answer panels via the open() and close() functions, but fails to set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons to indicate the expanded state. This violates the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern which requires aria-expanded to be toggled.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [faqAccordion.open]: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;`
  Line [faqAccordion.close]: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and linking error messages via aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [srUtils.markInvalid]: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Line [srUtils.markValid]: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` immediately after the aria-describedby cleanup block

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are registered, the trigger button receives aria-haspopup and aria-controls attributes, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" even though the panel is initially hidden. Screen readers assume the dropdown is expanded by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line [register function]: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is set up with event listeners during init, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load despite the navigation being closed by default. This causes screen readers to announce an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line [mobileNav.init]: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with role, aria-haspopup, and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the suggestion listbox is hidden on page load. This violates the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line [siteSearch.init]: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually, but screen reader users are not notified of the change in billing period or the new prices via a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [setPeriod function]: After the line `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));
  ```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers via the liveRegion utility, despite the functions having an announceToSR parameter that is never utilized in the function body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [faqAccordion.open]: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));` immediately before the `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` line
  Line [faqAccordion.close]: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));` immediately before the `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` line

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation has opened or closed, despite the functions having an announceToSR parameter.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [mobileNav.open]: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` immediately after opening the drawer (after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`)
  Line [mobileNav.close]: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately after closing the drawer (after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`)

issuesFound.title: contactForm inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for first name, last name, and email are personal data inputs that should have autocomplete attributes to help users fill forms, but the code does not set these attributes on the input elements when initialising the form.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field lacks an autocomplete attribute, which prevents browsers from assisting users with autofill for email addresses and violates WCAG 2.2 success criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [newsletterForm.init]: After the line `_initialised = true;`, add:

  if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the active class on filter tab buttons to show the selected category, but it does not update the aria-pressed attribute. These buttons function as toggle buttons where one is always pressed, so the state must be conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3005: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after determining isActive.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and linking error messages via aria-describedby, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true" for invalid fields or "false"/removes it for valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3410: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the else block (invalid case) before or after srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);. Line 3405: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); inside the if block (valid case) or use removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" when the page loads. Since the menu starts closed, the trigger must communicate this state to screen readers before any interaction occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2195: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after verifying _trigger and _drawer exist and before attaching event listeners.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up new dropdown instances and initializes the panel with hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button initially. Screen readers will not know the dropdown is collapsed until it is first toggled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setting setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); and before pushing to _instances.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch controller implements a combobox pattern but never sets the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl). The mount() function sets up aria-controls and role="combobox", but aria-expanded is required to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is displayed. The _open() and _close() functions also fail to toggle this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2570: Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); in the mount() function. Line 2605: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in the _open() function. Line 2615: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); in the _close() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product grid visibility, count badges, and empty states when filters change, but it does not announce the results to screen readers via a live region. Users who cannot see the screen are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2975: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Showing ${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products`); after _syncPagination(totalMatches); and before emitting the filter:applied event.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays and toggle states when users switch between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have updated or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2775: Add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly); after updating the plan cards and before emitting the pricing:periodchange event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, the mobileNav.open() and close() functions do not announce the state change to screen readers. Although focus is trapped inside the drawer when open, users are not informed via a polite live region that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2148: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); inside open() after _lockScroll();. Line 2168: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); inside close() after _unlockScroll();.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes dropdown panels but does not announce these state changes via a live region. When a user activates a dropdown trigger, they receive no confirmation that the menu has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2287: Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened'); inside _openInstance() after opening the panel. Line 2302: Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed'); inside _closeInstance() after closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the faqAccordion.open() and close() functions do not announce the state change via a live region. Although the accordion pattern typically moves focus to the panel or keeps it on the button, users benefit from confirmation that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3158: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded'); inside open() after opening the panel. Line 3178: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed'); inside close().

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init() function sets role="switch" on the billing toggle element (_toggleEl) when it is not a native button, but it never sets the aria-checked attribute to indicate the on/off state of the switch. Screen readers cannot convey whether annual or monthly billing is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')); after setting the role. Also update this attribute inside setPeriod() when the period changes: Line 2760: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(period === 'year'));.

issuesFound.title: focusTrap missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap() function manages focus for modal dialogs and drawers (such as the mobile navigation and cookie consent banner) but does not set aria-modal="true" on the container element. This attribute is necessary to inform screen readers that the content beneath the modal is inert and should not be navigable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: Add setAttr(container, 'aria-modal', 'true'); inside the createFocusTrap function after validating the container element.

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss without pause control
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.show() function automatically dismisses toast notifications after a timeout (default 4000ms) using setTimeout. There is no mechanism for users to pause, stop, or hide these notifications before the timeout expires, violating WCAG 2.2.1 (Pause, Stop, Hide) for auto-updating content that is not essential.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: Replace the simple setTimeout with a pausable timer that clears when the user hovers over or focuses the toast element, and restarts when focus/mouse leaves. Alternatively, remove auto-dismiss and require manual dismissal via the dismiss button already provided.

fpTitles: toastManager auto-dismiss without pause control

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but the parent FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with that ID. Screen readers will not announce the validation error message when it appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1918: Change `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm field error messages not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: All four FormGroup usages pass the error prop (which FormGroup does not accept) instead of errorMessage, and omit the errorId prop. Consequently, the ErrorMessage elements are not rendered with the IDs referenced by the TextInput and Textarea aria-describedby attributes, breaking error announcements for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2030: Change `error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}`
  Line 2045: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}`
  Line 2060: Change `error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}`
  Line 2075: Change `error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button loading state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component expects an isLoading prop to render the spinner and set aria-busy, but the code passes loading={isSubmitting}. The loading state is not communicated to assistive technology, and the spinner is not shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: Change `loading={isSubmitting}` to `isLoading={isSubmitting}`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button loading state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component expects an isLoading prop, but loading={isSubmitting} is passed instead. The button does not receive aria-busy or display the loading spinner, so screen reader users are not informed that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: Change `loading={isSubmitting}` to `isLoading={isSubmitting}`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly marked with aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog div. This incorrectly marks the backdrop as the modal element rather than the actual dialog, potentially causing assistive technologies to misidentify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the `<div className="modal-overlay"` and add `aria-modal="true"` to the `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog"` element on the next line.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm button aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The button has aria-describedby="newsletter-status", but the element with this ID only exists when status === 'error' or status === 'success'. When the form is idle (the button is visible and no status message is shown), the attribute references a non-existent ID, causing screen readers to ignore the describedby relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: Change `aria-describedby={statusId}` to `aria-describedby={status === 'error' ? statusId : undefined}` or remove the attribute entirely.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls to reference the submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the visibility state of the controlled menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element in NavDropdown.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu toggle button in the mobile navigation uses aria-controls but does not have aria-expanded to indicate the open/closed state of the submenu to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element inside the MobileNav component's submenu rendering logic.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button uses aria-controls to reference the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the panel is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon (sun/moon/computer) and lacks an aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires this prop, resulting in an empty accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} (or descriptive text indicating the action and current state) to the IconButton in ThemeToggle.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close button contains only the "✕" character and lacks an aria-label, resulting in no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button contains only the "‹" character and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button contains only the "›" character and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` boolean prop to style error states but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users when form validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element in TextInput.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` boolean prop for styling but does not map it to `aria-invalid`, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the textarea element in Textarea.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active state with CSS classes but do not use aria-pressed to communicate the selected/toggled state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button in the FILTER_TABS map.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly billing button.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual billing button.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link is visually styled as current but does not have aria-current="page" to indicate the current page to screen readers navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element in DesktopNav.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active mobile navigation link lacks aria-current="page" to indicate the current page to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text that follows in the VisuallyHidden span.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the asterisk in FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy, which would indicate that the element is being updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element in Button component.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links does not have an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element in SkipLinks.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The main desktop navigation lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from the mobile navigation landmark in screen reader landmark lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav element with id="main-nav".

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element that lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Mobile" to the nav element with className="mobile-nav__body".

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label to identify it in screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element in HeroSection.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with its visible heading (id="stats-heading"), making it harder to identify in landmark lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element in StatsBar.

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star symbols (★ and ☆) are not hidden from assistive technology. The container already has an aria-label describing the rating, so the individual stars are redundant and should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spans containing the star characters inside the map function in StarRating.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop overlay has an onClick handler to close the navigation but is implemented as a div without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot click.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space keys to the backdrop div, or change to a button element with appropriate styling.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay aria-modal misplaced and click handler not accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. Additionally, the overlay has an onClick handler without button semantics, though keyboard users can use the Escape key or close button.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Move aria-modal="true" to the dialog div (role="dialog"), and add role="button" and tabIndex={0} to the overlay div if it must remain clickable, or remove the onClick from the overlay.
